prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `9def23fe`.
codex_response:
`9def23fe` is implemented as the blocker-gated arm-extension task: find the solid square of `2`s, extend each square row left/right only when that whole side segment is empty, and extend each square column up/down only when that whole side segment is empty; the non-`2` singleton cells stay unchanged. I used the `opus46` hint as the starting hypothesis and rejected the `sonnet45` hint because it incorrectly described full-width row fills and interior stripe generation; that correction is recorded in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_9def23fe/notes.md#L1). No new shared primitive was needed.

The package is wired in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_9def23fe/__init__.py#L1), the DSL-style solver is in [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_9def23fe/verifier.py#L4), and the constructive generator is in [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_9def23fe/generator.py#L65). The generator matches the official family by sampling a square `2` block, one blocker color from `{1,3,4,8}`, and exactly eight purposeful singleton blockers split across the four side strips, then deriving the output from those blocked row/column sets instead of calling the verifier.

Validation passed: official examples `4/4`, generated examples `1000/1000`. Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/9def23fe/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/9def23fe/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/9def23fe/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/9def23fe/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/9def23fe/previews/preview_03.png). I also did a crude visual sanity check of `preview_01.png` via ASCII downsampling because no terminal image renderer was installed.
